steps.title: Identify the exponential form
steps.content: The function $f(t) = 34{,}000(2)^t$ has the standard exponential form $f(t) = a \cdot b^t$, where $a = 34{,}000$ is the initial value (output at $t = 0$) and $b = 2$ is the growth factor (the multiplier each hour).

steps.title: Evaluate at the initial measurement
steps.content:
Substitute $t = 0$:
$$f(0) = 34{,}000 \cdot 2^0 = 34{,}000 \cdot 1 = 34{,}000$$
Since $t$ is measured in hours after the initial measurement, $t = 0$ is the initial measurement. So $34{,}000$ represents the bacteria count at that starting moment.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: "The number of bacteria at the initial measurement" — matches $f(0) = 34{,}000$. Correct.
  • Choice B: "The number of hours for the population to double" — that is determined by the base $b = 2$, not by $34{,}000$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: "The number of bacteria 1 hour after the initial measurement" — that would be $f(1) = 34{,}000 \cdot 2 = 68{,}000$, not $34{,}000$. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: "The number of times the population doubles in 1 hour" — the population doubles once per hour, not $34{,}000$ times. Incorrect.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
